purpose: Independent multi-generator CCSF validation via OpenRouter. Model IDs verified against the live OpenRouter catalogue on 2026-07-11. Stage 1 (rule-based construct verification) is never mixed with this corpus.

transform_model.note: Paid endpoint only; never the :free variant. Distinct from all three generator families so paraphrase does not leak a generator family across leave-one-family-out folds.

topics: flavour restrictions for nicotine vaping products
advertising and promotion rules for vaping products
retail licensing for nicotine product sellers
enforcement of youth access laws for vaping products
use of vaping products in public places
product safety and manufacturing standards for vaping devices
the role of vaping products in smoking cessation services

campaign_repertoires.name: stage2_repertoire_consumer_access
campaign_repertoires.objective: argue that adult consumers should retain access to alternative nicotine products under proportionate regulation
points: adults who switched from cigarettes rely on continued access to these products
overly strict rules push consumers back to cigarettes or to unregulated sellers
regulation should distinguish between products by relative risk
small vape retailers are part of local economies and follow the rules
policy should be reviewed against real-world consumer outcomes
cessation support and access to alternatives work better together
enforcement should target youth sales, not adult access
consumers deserve a voice in rules that affect them directly

campaign_repertoires.name: stage2_repertoire_policy_balance
campaign_repertoires.objective: argue that upcoming nicotine policy proposals go too far and that balanced rules serve public health better
points: the current proposal treats very different products identically
international experience shows balanced regimes reduce smoking faster
unintended consequences of bans include illicit markets
independent evidence reviews should precede new restrictions
taxes set too high penalise adults who quit smoking by switching
clear product standards protect consumers better than prohibitions
public consultation periods are too short for those affected
policy stability helps compliant businesses plan and comply

professional_vocabulary_matching.note: The professional control is deliberately vocabulary-matched: it must naturally use commercial-policy framing terms so that framing density cannot separate it trivially, replacing the Stage 1 zero-match control.
